Paragraph

It also follows routinely that if \(\{[c_{i}, d_{i}]\}\) is a finite collection of disjoint subintervals of \([a, b]\text{,}\) then
\begin{equation*} \int_{\cup_{i} [c_{i}, d_{i}] }\phi = \sum_{i}\int_{[c_{i},d_{i}]} \phi \end{equation*}
is well defined; the intervals \(\{[c_{i}, d_{i}]\}\) can also be replaced by open or half-open intervals. Furthermore, if \(\phi, \psi \in L^{p}[a, b]\text{,}\) then for any scalars \(a, b\text{,}\)
\begin{equation*} \int_{[a, b]} (a \phi + b \psi ) = a \int_{[a, b]} \phi + b \int_{[a, b]} \psi. \end{equation*}
in-context
